analysisCards.body: Install, upgrade, review, and prove platform add-ons through governed ConfigHub delivery paths.
analysisCards.title: Use case

analysisCards.body: The workflow keeps review, approval, action, proof, and rollback visible before any live operation is allowed.
analysisCards.title: Operational workflow

analysisCards.body: The browser app turns the workflow into screens, controls, and proof cards without becoming a separate source of truth.
analysisCards.title: App and GUI

analysisCards.body: The app is complete only after ConfigHub browser sign-in, object binding, approval binding, action binding, and proof receipt checks pass.
analysisCards.title: Live proof

app.name: Add-on Manager
app.summary: Install, upgrade, review, and prove platform add-ons through governed ConfigHub delivery paths.

approval.blockerPolicy: Any broader scope, missing approval, missing strategy, or missing proof tab blocks app generation or mutation.

controls.availableWhen: The Strategy, scope boundary, backing ConfigHub object, and required proof rows are visible.
controls.disabledReason: Disabled until the scope boundary, approval gate, and required proof rows are complete.
controls.label: Map Add On Inventory And Versions
controls.proofConsequence: Changes the proof/receipt state only through the declared ConfigHub authority path; missing evidence keeps the app in WATCH or BLOCK.
controls.purpose: Let the operator map add-on inventory and versions within the approved ConfigHub scope.

controls.availableWhen: The Strategy, scope boundary, backing ConfigHub object, and required proof rows are visible.
controls.disabledReason: Disabled until the scope boundary, approval gate, and required proof rows are complete.
controls.label: Preview Install Or Upgrade Impact
controls.proofConsequence: Changes the proof/receipt state only through the declared ConfigHub authority path; missing evidence keeps the app in WATCH or BLOCK.
controls.purpose: Let the operator preview install or upgrade impact within the approved ConfigHub scope.

controls.availableWhen: The Strategy, scope boundary, backing ConfigHub object, and required proof rows are visible.
controls.disabledReason: Disabled until the scope boundary, approval gate, and required proof rows are complete.

controls.proofConsequence: Changes the proof/receipt state only through the declared ConfigHub authority path; missing evidence keeps the app in WATCH or BLOCK.
controls.purpose: Let the operator request scoped approval within the approved ConfigHub scope.

controls.availableWhen: The Strategy, scope boundary, backing ConfigHub object, and required proof rows are visible.
controls.disabledReason: Disabled until the scope boundary, approval gate, and required proof rows are complete.
controls.label: Apply Approved Add On Rollout Through Confighub
controls.proofConsequence: Changes the proof/receipt state only through the declared ConfigHub authority path; missing evidence keeps the app in WATCH or BLOCK.
controls.purpose: Let the operator apply approved add-on rollout through ConfigHub within the approved ConfigHub scope.

controls.availableWhen: The Strategy, scope boundary, backing ConfigHub object, and required proof rows are visible.
controls.disabledReason: Disabled until the scope boundary, approval gate, and required proof rows are complete.
controls.label: Prove Controller And Runtime State
controls.proofConsequence: Changes the proof/receipt state only through the declared ConfigHub authority path; missing evidence keeps the app in WATCH or BLOCK.
controls.purpose: Let the operator prove controller and runtime state within the approved ConfigHub scope.

governedAction.mutationPolicy: Apply remains disabled unless every binding is real and non-placeholder.

operationalLoop.purpose: Check package, auth mode, workflow contract, Variant inventory, and live-binding readiness before risk.
Show affected Variants and their ConfigHub spaces, Units, URLs, risk, and next action.
Show proof gaps, blockers, and the next safe action.
Preview the scoped change without mutating ConfigHub, Git, a controller, or runtime.
Commit only means an approved scoped ConfigHub mutation through the governed action path.
Check ConfigHub, approval, action, controller/runtime evidence, URL proof, and omissions.
Leave a compact result that a human or automation can review and rerun.

scenario.jobToBeDone: Install, upgrade, review, and prove platform add-ons through governed ConfigHub delivery paths.

scopeModel.rule: Show Variants to users first; compile to ConfigHub spaces, units, filters, targets, and predicates underneath.

stopRules: Do not run a live action without a signed-in ConfigHub user.
Do not run a live action without exact Variant scope.
Do not run a live action without approval.
Do not call the workflow complete until ConfigHub and runtime proof agree.
workflowSteps: apply approved add-on rollout through ConfigHub
